fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 36-year-old African American female with schizoaffective disorder for follow-up.
fields.Subjective: Patient presented appearing upbeat and well-groomed. Reports excellent medication adherence, taking paliperidone ER 6 mg each morning using phone alarm. States feeling great. No hallucinations for six months. No delusional thoughts. Sleep excellent at 8 hours nightly. Appetite good, weight stable at 168 lbs. Working full-time as medical records clerk at hospital, performing well. Mood 'really good' rated 8/10. Lives independently in apartment. Active in singles group at church. Recently started dating someone she met through mutual friends. Enjoys Zumba classes three times weekly. Attending therapy monthly for wellness. Five years sober from crack cocaine. Strong support from mother and church community. Feels life is on good track. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Seen by Dr. Kevin Washington, MD, Psychiatrist)
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Living independently; employed full-time; five years sober; dating; active in church and fitness; excellent recovery.
Assessment_and_Plan: Outstanding medication adherence and sustained remission.
Commended on sustained sobriety and career.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Kevin Washington, MD, Psychiatrist — Urban Wellness Psychiatry, 4521 Community Drive, Washington, DC 20001, Phone: (555) 229-7743, Fax: (555) 229-7744
